title: conmon no longer writes to syslog
body:
If the caller sets up the app to be in logrus.DebugLevel,
then we will add the --syslog flag to conmon to get all of the
messages.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
I don't know if we need a full With... for this. Does CRI-O not want
logging here? If they don't mind I would just make this unconditional.

On Wed, Jun 27, 2018, 15:56 Daniel J Walsh <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> Need to pass through the Syslog flag to conmon to get it to
> write information to syslog. We need this data to be able to debug
> issues like containers crashing.
>
> Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh dwalsh@redhat.com

> Does CRI-O not want logging here? If they don't mind I would just make this unconditional.

It was explicitly made conditional in kubernetes-incubator/cri-o#1623 (thank you `blame` ;).  Motivation is in kubernetes-incubator/cri-o#1604.

Alright, we'll need to follow that, then. And I was hopeful we could reduce
the diff a bit more in size.

On Wed, Jun 27, 2018, 16:04 W. Trevor King <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> Does CRI-O not want logging here? If they don't mind I would just make

> or mute the thread

Yes this was explicitly turned off for CRI-O.

> And I was hopeful we could reduce the diff a bit more in size.

The issue seems to be the number of layers you need to drill through to get from the libpod config down to `conmon` options.  In cases like this where libpod doesn't actually care about the `conmon` options, it might be easier to have a single `ConmonOptions []string` where callers can stuff in whatever they want.  Then we append the options libpod cares about after those.  That would be about the same size as this diff, and it would be a bit less structured/secure, but it would mean we didn't have to bump libpod code when folks want to twiddle another `conmon` option.

Another approach which avoids bumping libpod entirely would be to have a `debug-conmon.sh` like:

```sh
#!/bin/sh
conmon --syslog "$@"
```

and have the caller use `podman --conmon debug-conmon.sh ...` or whatever.

@wking @mheon Lot simpler patch, I now only check if libpod is running in logrus.DebugLevel, then it appends on the --syslog flag, and outputs a Debug message indicating conmon longs are in syslog.

This eliminates all the plumbing.  Only down side is I think conmon output would get logged twice by CRI-O in this situation.

> Only down side is I think conmon output would get logged twice by CRI-O in this situation.

Yeah.  And I'd prefer [not to conflate verbosity with the log channel][1].  So personally I prefer the previous iteration of this patch, but this one isn't going to crash or anything.  And folks are unlikely to run at debug level in production, so it's not terrible if that level has some quirks.

[1]: https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/pull/1014#discussion_r198857385
Latest rev LGTM, but ci tests aren't happy, looks like lint issues.
